topic: I make music about dependability. I have twin girls who just turned one. Here's what that pressure actually sounds like.

hooks.spoken_hook: Nobody talks about what it actually sounds like when you're terrified of letting your kids down. I put it in a song.
hooks.visual_hook: Close-up of two baby shoes side by side on a studio console, beat slowly fading in underneath
hooks.text_overlay: THIS IS WHAT PRESSURE SOUNDS LIKE
hooks.energy: quiet, heavy, intimate — pulls you in before you realize it

hooks.spoken_hook: People say music is an escape. Mine isn't. I make music about showing up every single day whether you feel like it or not.
hooks.visual_hook: Artist at the studio board, back to camera, no eye contact — slow turn halfway through the line

hooks.spoken_hook: I was in the booth at midnight. Twin girls asleep at home. I laid down a verse and cried after. That take made the album.
hooks.visual_hook: Dark studio, single light over the mic, artist walking into frame mid-sentence like the camera caught something real
hooks.energy: raw, cinematic, no performance — feels like a confession

hooks.spoken_hook: My daughters turned one. I have eleven songs finished about never letting them see me quit. That's the whole album.
hooks.visual_hook: Split frame — one side a birthday moment, other side a notebook of lyrics, cuts between both on the beat
hooks.energy: earned, deliberate, weight behind every word — no celebration, just accountability

hooks.spoken_hook: Hold on — before you keep scrolling, tell me the last time a rap song made you feel like you had to be better tomorrow.
hooks.visual_hook: Artist looks directly into camera and physically steps closer, like breaking the fourth wall mid-scroll
hooks.energy: disruptive but not loud — stops the thumb with directness, not chaos
